name: Toilet Porta Cabins
description:
Toilet Porta Cabin — Multi-Cubicle Sanitation Block, Factory-Built

A toilet porta cabin from SAMAN is a full MS corrugated steel cabin structure whose entire interior is configured as toilet cubicles — not a standalone individual unit, but a fixed sanitation block serving 25 to 200 construction site workers from one delivery, one foundation, and one site plumbing connection.

How Many Cubicles Does Your Site Need — Capacity by Worker Count

Single toilet porta cabin for small sites with up to 25 workers

Before ordering a toilet porta cabin, confirm the right cubicle count for your site worker strength. This table gives the answer before you call.

Worker Count Configuration Cabin Size
Up to 25 1-cubicle single unit 10×6 ft
25–50 2-cubicle block 10×10 ft
50–100 4-cubicle block 10×18 ft
100–150 6-cubicle block 10×24 ft
150–200 8-cubicle block 10×30 ft
200+ with gender segregation 4+4 split male/female 10×24 ft

Each row is aligned to the BOCW Act standard of 1 sanitation provision per 25 workers. A 4-cubicle SAMAN toilet porta cabin serving 100 workers replaces four separately deployed individual units — with one delivery address, one foundation, one manifold water connection, and one drainage outlet.

4-cubicle toilet porta cabin designed for 80–100 workers at construction sites

MS Corrugated Steel vs FRP — Why Material Matters for a Fixed Block

A toilet block serving 50 to 200 workers per day places demands on the structure that a lightweight event toilet never faces. Three specific requirements make IS 2062 MS corrugated steel the correct material for a fixed construction site toilet block — and make FRP the wrong choice at this scale.

Crane-liftability: A multi-cubicle toilet block must be relocated when the project advances or changes. An MS cabin block has 4-point crane lifting lugs welded to the IS 2062 base frame — the entire block lifts as one unit with all internal plumbing intact. An FRP toilet block at this scale cannot be crane-lifted as a single unit without structural modification to the frame.

Cubicle panel replaceability: In a 100-worker deployment, individual cubicle panels take daily mechanical impact. When an MS corrugated panel is damaged, a site fabricator cuts it out and welds in a new panel — Rs 800 to Rs 1,500 per panel. A cracked FRP panel at the same cubicle width requires a specialist composite repair or full panel replacement that disrupts the cubicle structure.

Structural load of 200 daily users: The floor, base frame, and door hinge mounting points in a toilet block see repeated impact load from 100 to 200 users daily. IS 2062 Grade A mild steel base frame and floor bearers are designed to carry this sustained load across a 20+ year service life. FRP shell construction is not rated for this sustained daily user count at fixed block scale.

For the full IS 2062 Grade A frame specification — frame dimensions, load capacity, and surface treatment process — our ms porta cabin page covers the complete material detail.

How the Plumbing Works — One Connection for the Entire Block

6-seater toilet porta cabin for 100–150 workers

A multi-cubicle toilet porta cabin uses a shared manifold plumbing system — one water inlet and one drainage outlet for the entire block, regardless of cubicle count.

A supply manifold pipe runs along the cabin ceiling space, branching to each cubicle's inlet valve from a single ½-inch main inlet at one end wall. One 200 to 400-litre overhead tank (size depends on cubicle count and daily user volume) feeds the entire manifold from a single bracket mounting on the exterior wall. Each cubicle's individual overhead tank is eliminated — the block functions from the shared manifold.

A shared PVC soil stack collects all cubicle drainage into one 110mm main drain pipe exiting at the cabin end wall. On site: one water supply pipe from your source to the block inlet, one drainage connection from the soil stack to your septic tank or bio-tank. No per-cubicle connections, no individual soak pits per cubicle, no separate water supply runs.

SAMAN provides a plumbing layout drawing confirming all connection points on your specific block at order confirmation — your civil contractor uses this to arrange site connections before delivery.

Gender-separated toilet porta cabin for large workforce sites

For buyers who need a supervisor's accommodation cabin with a single attached toilet rather than a multi-cubicle sanitation block, our porta cabin with toilet page covers the combined accommodation unit.

Toilet Cabin Block vs Standalone Units — Cost and BOCW Capacity

A SAMAN 4-cubicle toilet porta cabin: Rs 2,80,000 to Rs 3,50,000 delivered. Four individual standalone toilet units of equivalent capacity: Rs 1,80,000 to Rs 2,60,000. The cabin block costs 30 to 40% more.

What that premium delivers over the deployment period: one delivery instead of four, one concrete base instead of four, one manifold water connection instead of four individual connections, one drainage outlet instead of four separate soak pits, a shared handwash basin in the block's anteroom, internal LED strip lighting on a single electrical connection, and a 20+ year MS structural life versus 10 to 12 years for FRP standalone units.

On a 3-year deployment, the total cost of ownership — including maintenance, replacement of degraded FRP units at year 10–12, and repeated individual installation costs — favours the fixed cabin block from year 2 onwards.

BOCW capacity confirmation: A 4-cubicle SAMAN toilet porta cabin satisfies BOCW Act sanitation requirements for up to 100 workers at a single site. One inspectable fixed block replaces four individual unit deployments for welfare compliance documentation.

Modular toilet porta cabin installed in an urban environment

Frequently Asked Questions

Can a toilet porta cabin be crane-lifted and relocated to a new project site without being dismantled?

Yes. Every SAMAN toilet porta cabin is fabricated with 4-point crane lifting lugs welded to the IS 2062 base frame — positioned at the four corners of the cabin skid. The entire block including all internal fittings, plumbing manifold, and cubicle partitions can be lifted by crane as a single unit, loaded onto a flatbed truck, transported, and re-installed at a new site without any dismantling. Relocation time: 4 to 6 hours including disconnection, loading, and reconnection.

What septic tank or bio-tank capacity is recommended for a 4-cubicle toilet porta cabin serving 100 workers?

A 4-cubicle block serving 100 workers requires a minimum 3,000-litre capacity septic tank for a standard site deployment, or a 2,000-litre bio-digester tank if a bio-tank system is preferred. SAMAN provides the drainage outlet dimensions and flow rate confirmation for the block at order stage — your civil contractor uses these to size the septic or bio-tank correctly before the block arrives.

Can the cubicle count in an existing toilet porta cabin be increased by extending the cabin after delivery?

Yes, with one condition. The cabin must have been designed at the original order stage with a flush end-wall frame. If your original order confirms a flush end frame, a new cubicle extension section can be bolted to the existing end wall. If the original order did not specify an expansion end, the end wall must be cut and a structural connection welded — more expensive and disruptive. Confirm any planned future expansion at the order stage.

For the full SAMAN porta cabin range — all sizes, materials, delivery timelines, and rental options — see our porta cabins hub page.

Tell us your site worker count, gender segregation requirement, and site location.

We will confirm the right cubicle configuration, BOCW capacity, and delivery timeline within 24 hours. Delivery from our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities to sites across India in 7 to 21 working days.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
short_description:
🚻 Multi-cubicle sanitation block · IS 2062 MS steel · Crane-liftable · BOCW compliant

Toilet porta cabin — MS corrugated steel, factory-plumbed manifold system. 2 to 8 cubicles. Serves 25 to 200 workers. One delivery, one water connection, one drainage outlet.

Size
20′ × 10′ × 9′
Cubicles
2 to 8 · Gender Split Available
Delivery
7–21 Working Days
Plumbing
Shared Manifold · 1 Connection
Brand
SAMAN Portable
Best For
Construction Sites · 25–200 Workers

From Rs 2,80,000 · 4-cubicle = BOCW 100 workers · Crane-liftable base frame
